problem: How many degrees are in the measure of the smaller angle formed by the hour and minute hands of a clock when the time is 7 p.m.?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the position of the hour hand and the minute hand on the clock at 7 p.m.?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many degrees does the hour hand move in one hour? And how many degrees does the minute hand move in one hour?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the difference in degrees between the position of the hour hand at 7 p.m. and the position of the hour hand at 12?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the difference in degrees between the position of the minute hand at 7 p.m. and the position of the minute hand at 12?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can you use the differences in degrees to calculate the angle between the hour and minute hands at 7 p.m.?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any other times throughout the day where the angle between the hour and minute hands would be the same? If so, what are they?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other ways to determine the measure of the angle between the hour and minute hands at 7 p.m. on a clock?
classified_questions.label: e
